title: Bow Ties Are Cool Day
story: Bow ties get their moment to shine every August 28 on National Bow Tie Day! People everywhere dig out their fanciest bow ties or make silly ones from paper and ribbon. Some folks wear polka-dotted ones to work, while others post bow tie selfies online with hashtags like #BowTieSwag. Schools sometimes have bow tie contests where kids vote for the most creative design. Even pets get in on the fun with tiny bow ties attached to their collars! It's a day when everyone can feel a little more dapper and fancy, just like old-fashioned gentlemen from long ago.
activity_question: What do some people make bow ties out of on this holiday?
bonus_fact: The bow tie was invented because regular neckties kept falling into soup bowls!

title: Flaky, Sweet Cherry Turnover Treats!
story: Bakers across America fire up their ovens on August 28 for National Cherry Turnover Day! These golden pastries are like little pockets of happiness - flaky dough wrapped around sweet, juicy cherries. People celebrate by visiting bakeries, making homemade turnovers with their families, or trying new flavors like apple or blueberry. Some folks post photos of their perfectly golden creations on social media. The best part? You get to eat them warm with powdered sugar on top!
activity_question: What do people often sprinkle on top of warm cherry turnovers?
bonus_fact: Cherry turnovers are sometimes called "hand pies" because you can hold them!

title: Mouse Racing Madness Around Your Screen
story: Computer mice everywhere get their moment to shine on August 28 during Race Your Mouse Around the Icons Day! People celebrate by moving their computer mouse cursor as fast as possible around all the desktop icons on their screen. Some folks time themselves to see how quickly they can circle every folder, app, and shortcut. Others create fun obstacle courses using their icons, weaving the cursor between pictures and programs like a tiny digital race car. Kids especially love this silly holiday because it turns boring computer time into a game. Many people share videos of their mouse-racing skills online, and some even compete with friends to see who has the fastest cursor reflexes on 28 August each year!
activity_question: What do people move around their computer screen during this holiday?
follow_up_question: What creative mouse-racing course could you design using your desktop icons?
